commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8e61aafbd67da08b3606d2b4892856c3964b0989)
commands.review_summary:
The PR narrows Memory Wiki imported-source fs-safe error wrapping and adds a bridge regression for non-symlink write failures while trying to skip hardlink cleanup for non-files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current `writeImportedSourcePage` wraps every `FsSafeError` from the write path with symlink wording, and the PR's added test outlines a bridge import followed by a directory collision. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: This PR needs contributor real behavior proof and a small code fix before normal maintainer review; ClawSweeper repair should not take over while proof is mock-only.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): preserve fs-safe write diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve the symlink-specific imported source page refusal only for `symlink` and `path-alias` fs-safe failures.
- Surface the concrete fs-safe code/message for other imported source write failures.
- Limit hardlink source-page replacement to regular files so normal POSIX directories do not enter the hardlink cleanup path.

Fixes #83740.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Memory Wiki bridge imported-source writes previously converted every `FsSafeError` from the guarded write path into `Refusing to write imported source page through symlink`, even when the failure was not symlink-related.

Real environment tested:
Local macOS checkout at commit `8e61aafbd6` using the repository vitest wrapper and the bundled Node runtime.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
`PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-wiki/src/bridge.test.ts -t "reports non-symlink"`

Evidence after fix:
The regression creates a real bridge workspace and vault, imports a source page, replaces the generated source page with a directory, updates `MEMORY.md`, and reruns `syncMemoryWikiBridgeSources`.

Observed result after fix:
The rerun rejects with `Refusing to write imported source page ((not-empty|not-file|path-mismatch)): sources/...` and the same thrown message does not contain `through symlink`. Focused proof passed: 1 test passed, 8 skipped.

What was not tested:
I did not run a full end-to-end Memory Wiki plugin process outside vitest.

## Validation

- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-wiki/src/bridge.test.ts` - 9 passed
- `PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-wiki/src/source-page-shared.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/bridge.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`:
  `8e61aafbd6 Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> fix(memory-wiki): preserve fs-safe write diagnostics`
target.author: turbotheturtle
